commit.message:
fix: align print service implementation with Android PrintService API

- AkPrintService: add onConnected/onDisconnected lifecycle callbacks,
  track active jobs by ID (ConcurrentHashMap) for targeted cancellation,
  handle CancellationException separately in onPrintJobQueued, add
  setStatus() calls for user-visible progress, properly cancel in-flight
  coroutines in onRequestCancelPrintJob
- AkPrinterDiscoverySession: replace single trackingJob with trackingJobs
  map to support concurrent multi-printer state tracking, respect
  priorityList in onStartPrinterDiscovery by reporting priority printers
  first, add ensureActive() guard after connection probe, add printer
  description (type + address) to PrinterInfo, fix resource cleanup in
  onDestroy
- PrintJobProcessor: read document data and pageCount before state
  transitions to avoid access-after-state-change issues, add setStatus()
  progress updates throughout processJob, use block() state during retries
  per API contract, close pfd on connect failure to prevent resource leak,
  remove unused PrintJobInfo import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add .gitignore, remove cached pycache files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove dead code: unused methods, exports, and unreachable logic

- Remove has()/get() from ApprovalTraceStore (never called)
- Remove BRIDGE_TOKEN_PATH export (never imported)
- Remove DECKY_DIR_VALUE export (never imported)
- Remove getTheme() from layouts (never called)
- Remove roundedRect() and its unreachable fallback in approvalButtonSVG()
- Remove hasAnyColorOverrides() and .color-section CSS from PI (unused)
- Unexport internal-only symbols in config, state-machine, macro-exec,
  approval-trace, and layouts (types/constants used only within their module)
- Simplify always-constant widgetKind ternary in PI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(cli): use mcp-remote proxy format instead of type:http in generated .mcp.json

Claude Code's type:'http' requires OAuth2 fields (authorization_endpoint) that
our bearer-token auth doesn't provide. Switch to the mcp-remote npx proxy format
which is already proven to work.

commit.message:
Replace AdminPageBar with floating Manage widget and unified sidebar

- New AdminManageWidget: floating pill on view pages, opens collapsible left sidebar
- Unified PageEditSidebar with same UX: collapse/expand, view+edit links, tooltips
- Both sidebars share localStorage state — no flash when navigating between view/edit
- Sidebar pushes page content via body padding instead of overlaying
- Edit pages get serif heading with View Page button, plus AdminFooter
- Delete old AdminPageBar (bottom black bar)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix critical bugs, improve charts UX, and add LLM model picker

Backend fixes:
- Fix WebSocket bridge: add Redis pub/sub listener in main.py lifespan so
  live trade events actually reach connected clients
- Fix backtest candle fetch: paginate OHLCV calls to get full date range
  (~8760 candles for 1Y/1H) instead of capped 1000
- Fix strategy evaluate(): exit rules now only fire when in_position=True,
  entry rules only when in_position=False — prevents bull-market RSI>65
  from blocking buy signals on every candle (root cause of 0 trades)
- Fix live trading: use idx=len(df)-2 (last closed candle), close
  BinanceService per-symbol in finally block, remove service2 leak,
  log errors instead of swallowing them
- Wire LLM settings from DB: strategies compile endpoint and llm_task
  now read llm_provider/model/api_key/base_url from user_settings table
  with env fallback, so Settings UI changes take effect on next compile
- Move import logging to module level in llm_service.py

Frontend fixes:
- Fix charts "Object is disposed" crash: remove autoSize:true (caused
  ResizeObserver to paint after disposal), restore explicit width +
  resize handler, call chart.remove() in every effect cleanup
- Fix chart toolbar buttons not registering clicks: overflow:hidden on
  charts column prevents canvas from bleeding over the sidebar
- Add click-to-select and drag-to-move for horizontal price lines;
  Delete key or smart trash button removes selected line
- Add 30-min polling timeout on backtest hook; stops polling with error
  message if worker dies mid-run
- Add loading spinner on Live Trading page during initial data fetch
- Use stable React key for live trades table rows (trade.id fallback)
- Show "Key saved" badge on Settings fields when a secret is already stored
- Show toast on settings load failure instead of silent catch(() => {})
- Add per-provider LLM model quick-picker in Settings (Ollama/OpenAI/
  Anthropic presets) with editable text input for custom model names

Infrastructure:
- Add healthchecks for backend (curl /api/health) and worker (celery ping)
- Add .gitignore and .env.example

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: [CODE-FIX] Fix errors: Add workflow scope to GitHub PAT and com...

The underlying issue is a PAT scope limitation — the GitHub PAT for this
product has only `repo` scope; `workflow` scope is required to push files
under `.github/workflows/`. No workflow files exist in the repo and
CLAUDE.md already documents this blocker. No code changes are needed.

commit.message:
fix: await email sends in auth routes (Vercel serverless fix)

Vercel serverless kills the process after response is sent, so
fire-and-forget email sends never complete. Changed forgot-password,
resend-verification, and signup routes to await the email send
before returning the response.

feat: regenerate concept notes with enhanced template

- Added: quick stats (DataviewJS), related concepts discovery,
  use_case column, core questions checklist
- Removed: 容器化 (only 1 reference now)
- Added: 程式碼生成 (new concept with 2+ references)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: extract tenant scope from event annotations instead of hardcoding platform

The activity processor was ignoring scope annotations on Kubernetes events,
causing all event-sourced activities to be attributed to the platform scope
regardless of which project control plane emitted them.

Both BuildFromEvent and buildActivity now call ExtractTenantFromAnnotations
to read the platform.miloapis.com/scope.type and scope.name annotations
that Milo's events proxy injects based on the authenticated user's context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Migrate to TypeScript and upgrade amqplib and amqp-connection-manager to latest  (#25)

* Migrate from Flow to TypeScript with unit tests

- Convert Queue.js and index.js to TypeScript
- Add comprehensive unit tests (37 tests, 95%+ coverage)
- Replace Babel build with TypeScript compiler
- Add type declarations for amqp-connection-manager
- Generate .d.ts files for package consumers
- Maintain 100% backward API compatibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update amqplib and amqp-connection-manager to latest

- amqplib: ^0.7.1 → ^0.10.9
- amqp-connection-manager: ^2.3.0 → ^5.0.0
- @types/amqplib: ^0.5.17 → ^0.10.0
- Remove custom type declarations (now included in package)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add integration tests with real RabbitMQ

- Add docker-compose.yml with RabbitMQ 4.0.9
- Create 21 integration tests covering:
  - Connection handling and events
  - Job processing with concurrency
  - Error handling and retry logic (DLQ)
  - RPC call/response mechanism
  - Priority queues
  - Stop accepting jobs
- Add test utilities and setup scripts
- Update jest config to separate unit and integration tests
- Add npm scripts: test:integration, test:integration:watch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* v1.11.0

* Expand integration test coverage with 11 new tests

- Add Named Queues tests (3): add(name, data), process(name, handler), prefix verification
- Add Connection Events tests (2): connection:error emission, reconnection behavior
- Add Min Processing Time test (1): verify slow handlers aren't delayed beyond natural duration
- Add Edge Cases tests (5): empty payload, large payload (1MB+), unicode/special chars,
  concurrent add+process, multiple consumer load balancing
- Add close() stub method with TODO for proper connection cleanup
- Update docker-compose to use port 5673 to avoid conflicts with existing RabbitMQ instances

Total integration tests: 21 → 32

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Implement close() method and add name getter

- Add `name` getter for accessing queue name (needed by upstream projects)
- Implement `close()` method for proper AMQP connection cleanup:
  - Closes all consume channels
  - Closes main channel
  - Closes connection
  - Clears reply handlers and resets state
- Update mocks to support close() testing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* v1.11.1

* Update README.md

* Add GitHub Actions CI workflow and fix RPC reliability

CI:
- Run unit tests across Node.js 22, 24, 25
- Run integration tests with RabbitMQ via docker compose
- Typecheck and build validation
- Triggers on push/PR to master

Fixes:
- Await chan.addSetup() in process() so consumer is fully bound
  before process() resolves
- Use addSetup instead of runOnceForChannel for RPC reply queue
  so it survives channel reconnections (fixes flaky CI timeouts)
- Fix wait-for-rabbitmq script to use management API
- Fix test script sed pattern to capture full passed count

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* v1.11.2

---------

Co-authored-by: Valentin Khatsaiuk <val.khatsaiuk@ca.fctg.travel>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(runner): auto-trigger cleaner after scraper finishes

Scraper and cleaner ran on independent cron schedules, so raw data
could sit uncleaned for hours. Now runner.sh chains the cleaner
immediately after every scraper run (success or failure).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix duplicate entries after quick save+refresh

Daily and weekly entries are one-per-date, so after merging Gist and
localStorage, deduplicate by date keeping the entry with the highest ID
(most recently saved). Resolves the two-entry flash when Gist push
hasn't completed before the page is refreshed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

カット指示が消失するバグを修正

- addCut: クラウド同期後のマージで最新のlocalStorageを参照するよう修正（レースコンディション解消）
- deleteCut: 古いReact stateではなくlocalStorageから最新データを読むよう修正
- バックエンドPUT: cuts/propsがundefinedの場合は既存データを保持（全削除を防止）
- syncFromCloud/fetchFromCloud: mergeProjectDataSafeでマージし、ローカルのみのカットを保護
- 操作中ロック(operationInProgress)を追加し、CRUD中に自動同期が割り込まないよう制御
- 自動同期のガード時間を5秒→15秒に延長
- 共通ヘルパー関数(getLatestProjectsFromStorage, saveToLocalStorage)で一貫性確保

commit.message:
Add Watchtower web frontend for SENTINEL V2

New watchtower/ directory contains a complete React + Vite frontend:
- Setup wizard: prompts for SENTINEL server URL on first launch
- Live map dashboard: dark-themed Leaflet/OpenStreetMap with severity-coded markers
- Real-time event feed: SSE streaming, events appear instantly
- Advanced filters: source, category, severity, magnitude, full-text search
- Provider health panel: live status of all 24+ data providers
- Alert rules viewer: display configured alert rules
- Event detail panel: full metadata, badges, coordinates

Tech: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Leaflet + Tailwind CSS
No API keys needed — uses free OpenStreetMap tiles

Quick start: cd watchtower && npm install && npm run dev

fix: override qs and fast-xml-parser in remaining workspaces (#26692)

## Summary

- Bumps existing `qs` pnpm override from `^6.11.0` to `^6.15.0` in the
root workspace
- Adds new `qs: ^6.15.0` override in tools/api-markdown-documenter,
common/lib/common-utils, common/lib/protocol-definitions, and
common/build/eslint-plugin-fluid
- Adds `fast-xml-parser: ^4.5.4` override in the root workspace to
resolve multiple CVEs in 4.5.3 (entity encoding bypass, DoS via entity
expansion, stack overflow). Stays within @langchain/anthropic's declared
`^4.4.1` range.
- Adds `commentsOverrides` entries documenting overrides in all affected
workspaces
- Complements PR #26672 which covered build-tools, docs, and server
workspaces

## Test plan

- [x] CI passes — qs and fast-xml-parser are transitive dependencies,
minor/patch version bumps within declared ranges

- Removed unnecessary useState in Ticker
- Fixed animation fill-mode for slide-up
- Responsive fixes: company header, metrics grid, admin table, 52W range

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[consensus] Remove randomness fast path code (#18870)

* [consensus] Remove randomness fast path

The randomness fast path used a separate, higher secrecy threshold
(via ConfigV2) for faster randomness reconstruction. It was disabled
by default and the mainnet on-chain config was reverted from V2 to V1.
This removes all fast path runtime code while preserving backward
compatibility with BCS-serialized on-chain and local DB state.

Key decisions:
- Transcripts.fast and AugmentedData.fast_delta Option fields are
  retained (always None) for BCS deserialization compatibility
- Move ConfigV2 struct is kept (published on-chain type) but new_v2()
  is removed
- Key pair deserialization handles old format gracefully by falling
  through to regenerate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix formatting and rebuild cached packages

Apply cargo +nightly fmt and rebuild aptos-cached-packages after
removing new_v2() from randomness_config.move.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Return error on key pair deserialization failure instead of regenerating

Regenerating a new key pair on deserialization failure could cause a
mismatch with stale CertifiedAugData already persisted in storage,
leading to incorrect randomness shares. Instead, return an error to
disable randomness for the epoch, which is the safer behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Keep old key pair format for DB backward compatibility

Instead of migrating to a new serialization format, keep the old
(AugKeyPair, Option<AugKeyPair>) format and always write None for
the fast path key pair. This avoids any migration complexity and
ensures existing DB entries remain readable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Rebuild cached packages after rebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: keep rounding and Move code unchanged for safety

Per review feedback:
- Restore rounding code to original (pass None for fast path threshold
  instead of removing the parameter) to ensure replay safety since
  rounding is called inside VM during DKG transaction verification.
- Restore new_v2() in randomness_config.move since it's a published
  public function on the framework.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Rebuild cached packages after rebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Keep DKGPvssConfig.fast_wconfig for BCS backward compatibility

Retain the fast_wconfig: Option<SSConfig> field in DKGPvssConfig
(always set to None) to preserve BCS serialization compatibility,
consistent with the approach taken for Transcripts.fast and
AugmentedData.fast_delta.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: handle on-chain ConfigV2 gracefully

Fix tests from recent rebase that still referenced removed FastShare
type and old 4-argument verify() signature. Remove the fast share
sender verification test (no longer applicable) and update remaining
tests to use the new 3-argument verify().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting in DKGPvssConfig PartialEq

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: handle on-chain ConfigV2 gracefully

Keep ConfigV2 in OnChainRandomnessConfig enum for replay safety.
Historical DKG transactions may have used V2, and removing it from
TryFrom causes randomness_config_derived() to return None, which
would produce different DKGRounding parameters and break transcript
verification during blockchain replay.

V2 is now treated identically to V1: its secrecy_threshold and
reconstruction_threshold are used, but fast_path_secrecy_threshold
is ignored (fast path has been removed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Retain fast path secrecy threshold for rounding replay safety

Thread the fast_path_secrecy_threshold from on-chain ConfigV2 through
to DKGRounding::new, so that replay of historical V2-era DKG
transactions produces identical rounding parameters. For V1/Off
configs, None is passed (no fast path), preserving current behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Pass fast_wconfig from DKGRounding to DKGPvssConfig

Thread the fast_wconfig produced by DKGRounding through to
DKGPvssConfig instead of always using None. This ensures that
during replay of V2-era epochs, the DKGPvssConfig is constructed
with the same fast_wconfig as the original, matching the on-chain
BCS-serialized config exactly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Restore release builder V2 config and new_v2 constructor

Revert the removal of V2 variant from ReleaseFriendlyRandomnessConfig
and restore OnChainRandomnessConfig::new_v2() constructor. These are
needed for backward compatibility — removing them could break framework
upgrade compatibility checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting in build_dkg_pvss_config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Remove unused lru dependency

The lru crate was only used for the fast path broadcast cache
(blocks_with_broadcasted_fast_shares) which has been removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Update Cargo.lock for lru removal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

feat: Phase 6 — render pipeline, captions, aspect ratio + face tracking

Renderer:
- ffmpeg_builder: filter_complex from TimelineModel (concat, xfade, title cards)
- preview_renderer: libx264 ultrafast, 1280px for fast scrubbing
- final_renderer: NVENC with auto-fallback to libx264 slow
- queue_manager: RenderJob state machine + ffprobe duration

Caption engine:
- generator: timeline-relative captions from TranscriptionSegments
- styler: ASS header + events, word_highlight karaoke tags (\\kf)
- platform presets: tiktok (Impact/72pt), youtube, instagram
- hex_to_ass_color: correct &HAABBGGRR channel reversal
- burner: standalone ASS burn-in for re-render after edit

Aspect ratio:
- detector: MediaPipe face detection with graceful import fallback
- reframer: center-crop or face-centered crop → ffmpeg_vf string
- tracker: temporal smoothing (rolling avg) for stable reframing

Routers: /api/render/* (6 endpoints), /api/captions/* (8 endpoints)
RenderJob.render_params column added (auto-migrated on startup)

Implement AI provider switching and HA Voice Assistant setup

This commit completes several items from the implementation plan:

1. Fix JSON body construction in HA service call node

   - Use Object.assign() to merge entity_id with parameters
   - Remove fragile JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1) approach

2. Add AI provider switching capability (Phase 1.2)

   - Add "Route AI Provider" switch for provider selection
   - Add "Call Ollama" node for local LLM support
   - Add "Normalize AI Response" code node for response format handling
   - Support Zai GLM (default) and Ollama providers

3. Create comprehensive HA Voice Assistant setup guide

commit.message:
Launch Liri Vinyl Database — schema, contribution form, DB-first flip detection, flip notifications

Database (supabase/vinyl_schema.sql):
- vinyl_releases: one row per pressing/edition (catalog #, country, year,
  label, edition, version note, disc count, artwork, confirmed_count)
- vinyl_tracks: exact side positions (A1, A2, B1...) per release
- user_vinyl_collections: stub for future "tag your collection" feature
- RLS: public read, authenticated insert/update; auto updated_at trigger

add-vinyl.html (new page):
- 4-step flow: search iTunes → pressing details → side assignment → review/submit
- iTunes auto-populates artwork, tracks, year; user fills in pressing specifics
- Side assignment: ↑/↓ arrows move the FLIP divider between tracks per disc
- Multi-disc support: auto-groups by iTunes discNumber (LP1=A/B, LP2=C/D...)
- Writes to vinyl_releases + vinyl_tracks in Supabase on submit

index.html — vinyl mode upgrades:
- fetchVinylRelease: checks our DB first on every recognition
- getDbSideEndIndices: derives exact flip indices from DB track positions
- advanceToNextTrack now uses DB data > local learning > heuristic (priority order)
- "✓ In Liri's collection" badge shown when DB data is active
- Sides modal updated: shows "Not in Liri's collection yet" + link to add-vinyl.html
- Flip reminders settings: sound chime (Web Audio API) + push notification toggle
  - Sound: 3-note ascending chime via AudioContext (no file needed)
  - Push: Notification.requestPermission() with graceful denied state
- Both fire automatically when sideEndReason === "flip"

feat: AlertasFrotiX — TipoExibicao 9, Kendo TimePicker, gestão visual e controle de acesso

- Novo TipoExibicao 9 (Em Data Específica - dia inteiro): enum, controller, upsert JS/CSS
- Substitui ejs-timepicker por Kendo TimePicker (padrão do projeto) no Upsert
- Gestão de alertas: tabs reordenados (Ativos em 1º), cards ordenados por DataInsercao DESC
- Filtros de tipo por tab com badges coloridos (color-mix) e toggle ativo/inativo
- Border-left colorida por tipo de alerta (info, warning, success, primary, danger, aniversarios)
- Título no popup sino com cor do tipo (20% mais escuro via color-mix)
- Botão "Dar Baixa no Alerta" oculto por padrão — exibido apenas para criador/Admin via AJAX
- Performance: CarregarListas usa TOP 100 + projeção de colunas no DB (era GetAllAsync)
- Índices cobrindo criados: IX_Viagem_AlertaDropdown, IX_Manutencao_AlertaDropdown

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: modern-retro design overhaul (JetBrains Mono + wireframe style)

- Switch font to JetBrains Mono throughout
- Warm off-white background (#f5f0e8), almost-black text, deep red accent (#bf1a0a)
- Zero border-radius everywhere — sharp, geometric elements
- Wireframe buttons (.btn-wire) that invert to deep red on hover
- Bottom-border-only inputs (.input-line)
- Redesign HeroSection, StatusDashboard, AIAssistant, GlobalDiary to match
- High-contrast sidebar: inverted active nav item, thick left red border
